title: mapania pycnocephala subsp. fluviatilis
intro: Mapania pycnocephala subsp. fluviatilis is a plant species that belongs to the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. It is native to tropical regions and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, and is said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be administered orally to harness its medicinal properties.
